name: Airtable
description: Flexible cloud database with spreadsheet-like UI. Create and query records, manage bases, and build relational databases.
author: OpenJiuwen
desc_mk: Airtable is a flexible cloud database that combines the simplicity of a spreadsheet with the power of a relational database. Use this plugin to create, read, update, and delete records across your bases. **Note:** Get your API token at [Airtable Account](https://airtable.com/create/tokens). You will need your base ID (starts with `app`) and table name or ID for most operations.

Authorization.description: Airtable personal access token (Environment variable: AIRTABLE_API_KEY)

tools.name: List Records
tools.description: List all records in a table with optional filtering, sorting, and pagination

base_id.description: The ID of the Airtable base (starts with 'app', e.g. appXXXXXXXXXXXXXX)

table_id_or_name.description: The ID or name of the table to list records from

filterByFormula.description: Airtable formula to filter records. Example: NOT({Name} = '')

maxRecords.description: Maximum total number of records to return

view.description: The name or ID of a view to use for filtering and sorting

fields.description: Array of field names to include in the response. If empty, all fields are returned

offset.description: Pagination offset from a previous response to fetch the next page

tools.name: Get Record
tools.description: Retrieve a single record from an Airtable table by its record ID

base_id.description: The ID of the Airtable base (starts with 'app')

table_id_or_name.description: The ID or name of the table containing the record

record_id.description: The ID of the record to retrieve (starts with 'rec')

tools.name: Create Records
tools.description: Create one or more new records in an Airtable table (up to 10 records per request)

base_id.description: The ID of the Airtable base (starts with 'app')

table_id_or_name.description: The ID or name of the table to create records in

records.description: Array of record objects to create. Each object must have a 'fields' property. Example: [{"fields": {"Name": "Alice", "Status": "Active"}}]

typecast.description: Automatically convert string values to the appropriate field type

tools.name: Update Record (PATCH)
tools.description: Update specific fields of an existing record, leaving other fields unchanged

base_id.description: The ID of the Airtable base (starts with 'app')

table_id_or_name.description: The ID or name of the table containing the record

record_id.description: The ID of the record to update (starts with 'rec')

fields.description: Object containing the fields to update and their new values. Example: {"Name": "Updated Name", "Status": "Done"}

tools.name: Update Record (PUT)
tools.description: Replace all fields of an existing record, clearing any fields not included in the request

base_id.description: The ID of the Airtable base (starts with 'app')

table_id_or_name.description: The ID or name of the table containing the record

record_id.description: The ID of the record to replace (starts with 'rec')

fields.description: Object containing all fields for the record. Unspecified fields will be cleared. Example: {"Name": "Full Name", "Email": "email@example.com"}

tools.name: Delete Record
tools.description: Permanently delete a single record from an Airtable table

base_id.description: The ID of the Airtable base (starts with 'app')

table_id_or_name.description: The ID or name of the table containing the record

record_id.description: The ID of the record to delete (starts with 'rec')

tools.name: List Bases
tools.description: Return a list of all Airtable bases accessible to the authenticated user

offset.description: Pagination offset from a previous response to fetch the next page of bases

tools.name: Get Base Schema
tools.description: Return the schema of all tables in a base, including field definitions and views

base_id.description: The ID of the Airtable base to get the schema for (starts with 'app')
